message:
fix(engine): make requires_declassification fail-closed on missing authorization material

FlowAuthorizationAssessment::requires_declassification() previously only
returned true when a concrete DeclassificationObligation had been
materialized. Flows that were blocked by missing authorization (emitting
ExplicitAuthorizationRequired or DeclassificationObligationRequired
advisories) reported requires_declassification() == false even though the
flow still needs declassification follow-up before it can proceed.

This is a correctness issue: callers checking requires_declassification()
to gate declassification workflows would skip flows that actually need
attention.

Changes:
- Extend requires_declassification() to also return true when any advisory
  is ExplicitAuthorizationRequired or DeclassificationObligationRequired
- Update all affected test assertions (unit, edge case, integration) from
  assert!(!requires_declassification()) to assert!(requires_declassification())
- Add new test flow_assessment_keeps_non_declassification_flows_clear to
  verify that Public->OpenSink flows (which need no declassification) still
  return false

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
